message.reasoning:
We need to respond JSON with kind? Currently we are in a state of gathering evidence before editing. The description says: "Gather the exact evidence needed before editing schema definitions so fixture, task, evidence, trace, score, and run artifacts stay versioned and aligned." That's the objective. The strategy says expand_file priority 112. We have already done expand_file tool call and got tool_result? In history we see tool_result session.expand_file@v1 {status:ok,...}. Then hydrating source for artifacts.rs with session.hydrate@v1 etc. It seems we have already performed those actions. The latest info: tool_result session.hydrate@v1 {status:ok,...}. So we likely have evidence visible. Now we need to decide next action: maybe we should describe the graph or get some facts about schema version constants. We need to gather exact evidence needed before editing schema definitions. Likely need to read specific symbols like RUN_MANIFEST_SCHEMA_VERSION, TASK_SPEC_SCHEMA_VERSION, etc. We can use graph.describe or graph.find to get details. Probably we need to read the definitions of those constants to know their current values. Use graph.describe with selector for those symbols.

Thus we should issue a tool_call to graph.describe for maybe "RUN_MANIFEST_SCHEMA_VERSION"? But we need short ids. The graph shows nodes like S18 etc. Those are short ids? The graph shows [F1] ... --exports--> [S18] symbol:crates/graphbench-core/src/artifacts.rs::RUN_MANIFEST_SCHEMA_VERSION. The short id likely is S18. Similarly S30 is TASK_SPEC_SCHEMA_VERSION. So we can use graph.describe with selector "S18" or maybe the full symbolic name? The tool expects a selector string; likely can be the short id. So we can call graph.describe with payload {"selector":"S18"}.

We need to output JSON with kind: tool_call, tool_name: graph.describe@v1, payload. Also acquired_fact_ids list containing maybe "S18". readiness_state: evidence_acquired? Since we are about to call tool, not ready yet? The schema says readiness_state can be "not_ready|evidence_visible|evidence_acquired|ready_to_edit". After we get evidence, maybe we set evidence_acquired? But we haven't yet acquired the fact; we are about to request it. So readiness_state probably "not_ready". But maybe we can set "evidence_visible"? We have evidence visible from previous expansions? The instruction says evidence_visible when evidence is visible but not yet acquired? Not sure. Probably we set "evidence_visible" when we have evidence visible in context. We have evidence visible after expansions, but we need exact facts. So maybe we are "evidence_visible". But the readiness state likely indicates whether we are ready to edit. Not yet. I'd set "evidence_acquired" after we get the description? Actually after we get the description we will have the fact. So prior to calling tool, we are not ready; we need to fetch. So readiness_state: "not_ready". The JSON should include "assistant_message":"<brief status>" maybe "awaiting schema version details". Let's produce:

{
  "kind":"tool_call",
  "assistant_message":"Requesting schema version definitions",
  "tool_call":{"tool_name":"graph.describe@v1","payload":{"selector":"S18"}},
  "acquired_fact_ids":[],
  "readiness_state":"not_ready"
}

But we may also want to request multiple? Could request both S18 and S30 in one call? graph.describe can take a selector string; maybe we can request a list? Not sure. Could use graph.find with logical_key_regex to get both. Simpler to request one at a time. But we might want to get both. Could use graph.describe with selector "S18,S30"? Not sure if allowed. Probably just one.

 better to request "S18" first, get its description, then later request others. So respond with that.

Thus final JSON.
